objective: Apply a fork-only production hotfix so the default image/presets boot with Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B by pinning a language_model_only-capable vLLM build and adding a clear fail-fast guard for incompatible runtimes.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible changes in this fork only to Dockerfile, docker-bake.hcl, src/engine_args.py, scripts/config_doctor.py, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, README.md, and aiwf.request.json.
acceptance_criteria: Default image pins stable vLLM to 0.16.1 (deterministic) so Qwen3.5 text-only path (`LANGUAGE_MODEL_ONLY=true`) is available.
Build configuration exposes VLLM_VERSION as explicit pinned build arg for controlled rollback/override.
Engine arg parsing fails fast with an actionable error when Qwen3.5 is selected but runtime lacks language_model_only support or it is disabled.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this hotfix commit to restore the previous vLLM pin and remove the Qwen3.5 fail-fast guard. If partial rollback is needed, set `VLLM_VERSION=0.16.0` at build time and switch production presets away from `Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B` until a compatible image is redeployed. Operational rollback is pinning endpoint image to the last known-good fork tag while rebuild/redeploy completes.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only in staged rollout (1->2->4 workers). At each stage verify cold start, successful model load, and one representative inference before scaling.
threat_model: Main risks are behavior changes from vLLM version shift and false-positive startup blocks from stricter Qwen3.5 validation. Mitigation: pin exact stable version (`0.16.1`), keep changes narrow to startup argument validation, expose build arg for controlled rollback, and require staged runtime validation.
